title: Race-Query Ranking Objective Instead Of Pure BCE
summary: A pit call is relative to the other candidate laps in the same driver-race context.
rationale: A pit call is relative to the other candidate laps in the same driver-race context. Ranking objectives are built for within-group ordering and CatBoost explicitly supports groupwise losses such as QuerySoftMax, YetiRank, and QueryAUC-style evaluation.
implementation_hint: Define groups as driver-race or driver-race-stint, train a CatBoost ranker with QuerySoftMax or YetiRankPairwise on the same features, and compare OOF race-group AUC against the current classifier. Use the ranker as an ensemble member or as a feature feeding the probabilistic model.
expected_effect: Often helps when the main failure mode is picking the wrong lap inside the correct pit window rather than missing the window entirely.
risk: Groups with very few positives or long stretches of all-zero labels can make ranking unstable; bad grouping choices can hurt more than help.
